questions.question: Why isn't a text prompt enough to control image generation?
options: Text pins down only ~10% of an image's specification — pose, layout, perspective, and spatial relations are visual signals that can't be described efficiently in words
questions.explanation: Hence the toolkit: ControlNet for spatial structure, LoRA for new concepts/styles, IP-Adapter for image-as-reference — production pipelines layer several of each over an SDXL/SD3/Flux base.

questions.question: How does ControlNet steer a frozen diffusion model?
options: Clone the U-Net's encoder half, train the clone to read an extra conditioning input (depth, pose, edges), and feed its features into the frozen original's decoder via zero-convolutions — 1×1 convs initialized to zero so it starts as a no-op
It edits the text prompt automatically
questions.explanation: Zero-init means no harm before training; the clone learns a pure delta. Per-modality ControlNets are small side models (~360M for SDXL) and compose additively at inference with per-control weights.

questions.question: How does LoRA shrink fine-tuning by orders of magnitude?
options: Freeze each weight matrix W and learn a low-rank delta ΔW = B@A with rank r << d — 2·d·r new params instead of d²; for SDXL attention (d=640, r=16) that's 20k per adapter vs 410k, and a whole LoRA is 20-200MB vs the 5GB base
questions.explanation: Rank 4-16 is standard for attention, 64-128 for heavy fine-tunes. At inference the delta scales with α (0.5-1.5 typical) and multiple LoRAs stack additively — with nonlinear interactions as the caveat.

questions.question: What does IP-Adapter add that ControlNet and LoRA don't?
options: IMAGE-as-conditioning without training: a ~20MB adapter encodes a reference image via CLIP into tokens injected alongside text in cross-attention — "generate in the style of this reference" with no LoRA training
questions.explanation: The division of labor: ControlNet controls WHERE (spatial structure), LoRA controls WHAT/WHO (learned concepts), IP-Adapter controls LOOK (reference style) — composable on one frozen base.

questions.question: Why is the zero-convolution initialization critical to ControlNet?
options: Starting as an exact identity means the pretrained model's behavior is untouched at step zero — training can only learn a useful delta, never destabilize the frozen backbone it nudges
Zero convs run faster on GPUs
questions.explanation: It's the same safety pattern as Flamingo's tanh-gated cross-attention: bolt new machinery onto a frozen giant in a way that provably starts as a no-op.
